bids.reasoning: At the opening with no opponent offers or messages, I target my maximum-utility bid and avoid conceding before seeing their priorities. Since time pressure is zero, there is no need to trade away value yet, especially on the high-weight issues C and D.

At t=0.02 it is far too early to make major concessions, and the opponent’s only offer is all valueA, which is very low for my utility and shows no meaningful concession yet. I will avoid repeating my exact opening bid by making a small concession on the lowest-weight issueA while holding firm on the high-weight issuesC and issueD and important issueB.

I am targeting a high-utility early-game bid, conceding only on the lowest-weight issueA while holding firm on the more important issues B, C, and D. The opponent has repeated the same all-valueA offer, which is very poor for me and shows no real concession despite their urgency messages; at t=0.04 there is no need to make major concessions yet.

I am targeting a high-utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.06 and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeating the same low-value offer for me. Their urgency messages are cheap talk and contradict the actual early time fraction, so I will make only a small concession on my lowest-weight issue while holding firm on the more important issues.

At t=0.16 it is still early, so I am targeting a strong utility bid while making a modest concession to keep movement visible. At t=0.20 it is still relatively early, so I am not accepting the opponent’s low-utility bids or their repeated urgency messages at face value.
